title: Quel temps fait-il ? — Weather & small talk
objective: Talk about the weather and seasons and make simple small talk (A1: can describe today's weather and the seasons in short, simple phrases).

bridge.title: Bridge from Hindi / English: French says « il fait chaud » = literally “it makes hot”
bridge.body: To describe the weather, French uses **il fait** + adjective: **il fait chaud** (it's hot), **il fait froid** (it's cold), **il fait beau** (it's nice), **il fait mauvais** (it's bad weather). Word-for-word this is “it makes hot”, not “it is hot”. Hindi says गरमी है / ठंड है and English says “it IS hot” — so treat **il fait** as a fixed weather phrase to memorise, an analogy not a rule. For rain and snow, French uses one verb: **il pleut** (it's raining), **il neige** (it's snowing). To ask, use **quel** (masc.) / **quelle** (fem.): **Quel temps fait-il ?** = “What's the weather like?”

quiz.prompt: What is the main bridge idea for "Quel temps fait-il ? — Weather & small talk"?
options: Focus on: « il fait chaud » literally means “it makes hot”
Weather always uses the verb « être » like English
French has no word for rain

example.french: Quel temps fait-il à Mumbai aujourd'hui ?
example.englishGloss: What's the weather like in Mumbai today?

glosses.hi: आज मुंबई में मौसम कैसा है?
glosses.en: What's the weather like in Mumbai today?
glosses.pa: ਅੱਜ ਮੁੰਬਈ ਵਿੱਚ ਮੌਸਮ ਕਿਹੋ ਜਿਹਾ ਹੈ?

example.french: Aujourd'hui il fait beau à Delhi.
example.englishGloss: Today the weather is nice in Delhi.

glosses.hi: आज दिल्ली में मौसम अच्छा है।
glosses.en: Today the weather is nice in Delhi.
glosses.pa: ਅੱਜ ਦਿੱਲੀ ਵਿੱਚ ਮੌਸਮ ਵਧੀਆ ਹੈ।

example.french: En été, il fait très chaud à Delhi.
example.englishGloss: In summer, it is very hot in Delhi.

glosses.hi: गर्मियों में दिल्ली में बहुत गरमी होती है।
glosses.en: In summer, it is very hot in Delhi.
glosses.gu: ઉનાળામાં દિલ્હીમાં ખૂબ ગરમી હોય છે.
glosses.pa: ਗਰਮੀਆਂ ਵਿੱਚ ਦਿੱਲੀ ਵਿੱਚ ਬਹੁਤ ਗਰਮੀ ਹੁੰਦੀ ਹੈ।

example.french: En hiver, il fait froid à Delhi le matin.
example.englishGloss: In winter, it is cold in Delhi in the morning.

glosses.hi: सर्दियों में सुबह दिल्ली में ठंड होती है।
glosses.en: In winter, it is cold in Delhi in the morning.
glosses.gu: શિયાળામાં સવારે દિલ્હીમાં ઠંડી હોય છે.
glosses.pa: ਸਰਦੀਆਂ ਵਿੱਚ ਸਵੇਰੇ ਦਿੱਲੀ ਵਿੱਚ ਠੰਢ ਹੁੰਦੀ ਹੈ।

example.french: Il pleut beaucoup pendant la mousson à Mumbai.
example.englishGloss: It rains a lot during the monsoon in Mumbai.

glosses.hi: मुंबई में मानसून के दौरान बहुत बारिश होती है।
glosses.en: It rains a lot during the monsoon in Mumbai.
glosses.gu: મુંબઈમાં ચોમાસા દરમિયાન ખૂબ વરસાદ પડે છે.
glosses.pa: ਮੁੰਬਈ ਵਿੱਚ ਮੌਨਸੂਨ ਦੌਰਾਨ ਬਹੁਤ ਮੀਂਹ ਪੈਂਦਾ ਹੈ।
glosses.ta: மும்பையில் பருவமழை காலத்தில் அதிக மழை பெய்யும்.

example.french: Priya aime le chai quand il y a la mousson.
example.englishGloss: Priya likes chai when there is the monsoon.

glosses.hi: प्रिया को मानसून के समय चाय पसंद है।
glosses.en: Priya likes chai when it is monsoon season.
glosses.pa: ਪ੍ਰਿਯਾ ਨੂੰ ਮੌਨਸੂਨ ਵੇਲੇ ਚਾਹ ਪਸੰਦ ਹੈ।

example.french: En été, Arjun boit beaucoup d'eau.
example.englishGloss: In summer, Arjun drinks a lot of water.

glosses.hi: गर्मियों में अर्जुन बहुत पानी पीता है।
glosses.en: In summer, Arjun drinks a lot of water.
glosses.gu: ઉનાળામાં અર્જુન ખૂબ પાણી પીવે છે.
glosses.pa: ਗਰਮੀਆਂ ਵਿੱਚ ਅਰਜੁਨ ਬਹੁਤ ਪਾਣੀ ਪੀਂਦਾ ਹੈ।

example.french: En hiver, il neige à Shimla mais pas à Chennai.
example.englishGloss: In winter, it snows in Shimla but not in Chennai.

glosses.hi: सर्दियों में शिमला में बर्फ गिरती है पर चेन्नई में नहीं।
glosses.en: In winter, it snows in Shimla but not in Chennai.
glosses.mr: हिवाळ्यात शिमल्यात बर्फ पडतो पण चेन्नईत नाही.
glosses.gu: શિયાળામાં શિમલામાં બરફ પડે છે પણ ચેન્નઈમાં નહીં.
glosses.pa: ਸਰਦੀਆਂ ਵਿੱਚ ਸ਼ਿਮਲੇ ਵਿੱਚ ਬਰਫ਼ ਪੈਂਦੀ ਹੈ ਪਰ ਚੇਨਈ ਵਿੱਚ ਨਹੀਂ।
glosses.ta: குளிர்காலத்தில் ஷிம்லாவில் பனி பெய்யும் ஆனால் சென்னையில் இல்லை.
glosses.te: శీతాకాలంలో సిమ్లాలో మంచు కురుస్తుంది కానీ చెన్నైలో కాదు.
glosses.kn: ಚಳಿಗಾಲದಲ್ಲಿ ಶಿಮ್ಲಾದಲ್ಲಿ ಹಿಮ ಬೀಳುತ್ತದೆ ಆದರೆ ಚೆನ್ನೈನಲ್ಲಿ ಇಲ್ಲ.

exercises.hint: Remember: French weather uses « il fait » + adjective.
exercises.coachNote: Talk about the weather and seasons and make simple small talk (A1: can describe today's weather and the seasons in short, simple phrases).
exercises.prompt: How do you say “it is hot” about the weather in French?
exercises.explanation: Weather takes « il fait » + adjective, literally “it makes hot”, not « il est ».

exercises.hint: Same fixed weather verb as « il fait chaud ».
exercises.coachNote: Talk about the weather and seasons and make simple small talk (A1: can describe today's weather and the seasons in short, simple phrases).
exercises.text: En hiver à Delhi, il {{}} froid.

exercises.hint: Rain uses one verb, not « il fait ».
exercises.coachNote: Talk about the weather and seasons and make simple small talk (A1: can describe today's weather and the seasons in short, simple phrases).

exercises.hint: Start with the question word « Quel ».
exercises.coachNote: Talk about the weather and seasons and make simple small talk (A1: can describe today's weather and the seasons in short, simple phrases).
exercises.prompt: Build the question: “What's the weather like?”

exercises.hint: Match each French weather phrase to its English meaning.
exercises.coachNote: Talk about the weather and seasons and make simple small talk (A1: can describe today's weather and the seasons in short, simple phrases).
exercises.prompt: Match the weather phrase to English.

exercises.hint: « quel » agrees with the noun's gender.
exercises.coachNote: Talk about the weather and seasons and make simple small talk (A1: can describe today's weather and the seasons in short, simple phrases).
exercises.prompt: « saison » is feminine. Which asks “Which season?”
exercises.explanation: « saison » is feminine and singular, so use « quelle ».

exercises.hint: Recycle « il y a » = there is.
exercises.coachNote: Talk about the weather and seasons and make simple small talk (A1: can describe today's weather and the seasons in short, simple phrases).
exercises.text: À Mumbai en juillet, il y a la {{}} et il pleut beaucoup.

exercises.hint: Use « il fait » + adjective for “nice weather”.
exercises.coachNote: Talk about the weather and seasons and make simple small talk (A1: can describe today's weather and the seasons in short, simple phrases).
exercises.prompt: Aujourd'hui il fait beau à Delhi.
exercises.answer: Today the weather is nice in Delhi
accept: today it is nice in delhi
today the weather is nice in delhi
